qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via netAssets2LocalAssets using unsafe HTTP client without private-IP filtering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns SSRF in POST /api/format/netAssets2LocalAssets through util.NewCustomReqClient without private-IP filtering, while the advisory concerns double URL decoding in kernel/server/serve.go serveExport() for /export/ path traversal file reads.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described /export/ double URL decoding path traversal in serveExport() or the missing subpath validation that permits authenticated reads of workspace files such as temp/siyuan.db.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hjh7-r5w8-5872 describes kernel/server/serve.go serveExport(), where an extra url.PathUnescape() decodes %252e%252e into .. before filepath.Join under the /export/ handler.
The only final qualifying finding, ssrf-net-assets-to-local, identifies POST /api/format/netAssets2LocalAssets and kernel/model/assets.go using util.NewCustomReqClient without private-IP filtering.
The SSRF finding does not reference /export/, serveExport(), double URL decoding, filepath.Join traversal, or arbitrary workspace file reads through the export endpoint.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was produced, so runtime tiering does not apply.
